articles.title: Ask HN: Is there any tool that can stop LLM calls at runtime (not just monitor)?
articles.summary: I’ve been running into cases where LLM/agent systems make unexpected or repeated calls and costs spike quickly.Most tools I’ve found focus on observability (logs, traces, dashboards), but not actual enforcement.Is there anything that can:- stop or cut off a call mid-execution (based on budget, tokens, or conditions)?- enforce limits at runtime instead of just alerting after the fact?Curious if people here are solving this in practice, or just handling it at the application level. Comments URL: h

articles.title: Show HN: When Seeing Isn't Enough: Rescuing Stuck LLM Agents
articles.summary: Hi HN, we are sharing our latest preprint from CAUM Systems. We found that LLM coding agents enter 'blind-spot failures' that are completely unrescuable by behavioral nudging, but 100% rescuable by adding a single sentence of causal interpretation about structural data. We introduce the 'bandwidth of observer' framework and explain why cross-model evaluation is critical to tell blind-spots apart from capability floors. Happy to answer questions! Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id

articles.title: LLM plays an 8-bit Commander X16 game using structured "smart senses"
articles.summary: I connected the ChatGPT API (model gpt-4o) to an 8-bit shoot-'em-up game, PvP-AI, running on a Commander X16 emulator.Instead of pixels or audio, the model receives structured text summaries, what I’m calling "smart senses", based on the game's existing touch and EMF-style inputs.The LLM maintains notes between turns, develops strategies across games, and even discovers an exploit in the built-in AI's behavior.Write-up, technical details, and three sequential gameplay recordings: https://pvp-ai.

articles.title: Show HN: Heron is open-source security auditor that interviews your AI agents
articles.summary: Last week security guy in our company asked me to write doc with information about how they work, which data and systems it touches for all my AI agents. "It's too much work" i thought. So i built Heron. It interviews your agent with 10 main questions and smart follow-ups if feel uncertainty. It also has regulatory flags (SOC2, GDPR, EU AI Act etc). As a result you get a full report with all needed information.Feel free to try. No SDK, no code changes, MIT licensed. Have 4 modes (server, overrid

articles.title: Ask HN: Should AI credits be refunded on mistakes?
articles.summary: Something I’ve noticed (I’m using Claude subscription so no refunds but it applies to usage windows) is that sometimes AI makes mistakes so if something is important I tell Claude code to spin up a couple of sub agents and verify the information, often there will be a mistake and it gets rectified.It feels unfair I have to pay (or lose some usage) for this.Interested in other people’s thoughts. Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=47688712 Points: 3 # Comments: 4
